frameworks.name: Blind/anonymized screening practices
frameworks.citation: Bohnet (2016), What Works: Gender Equality by Design

dimensions.name: Job-analysis grounding
dimensions.evidence: Job tasks derived from a documented job analysis with SME sign-off.

findings.text: Job analysis not refreshed in 18 months

dimensions.name: Sourcing reach & diversity
dimensions.evidence: Three channels used; no outreach to women-in-tech communities; applicant pool skew flagged.

dimensions.name: Screening validity
dimensions.evidence: Unstructured resume keyword filter; no validated predictor weighting.

dimensions.name: Interview structure
dimensions.evidence: Behaviorally-anchored questions used; two interviewers; scoring rubric present.

dimensions.name: Bias-mitigation controls
dimensions.evidence: No blind screening; interviewer demographic skew; no calibration.

dimensions.name: Adverse-impact monitoring
dimensions.evidence: No four-fifths analysis run; selection rates by group not tracked.

dimensions.name: Candidate experience
dimensions.evidence: NPS survey sent post-decision; response rate 28%; no closing of loop.

dimensions.name: Decision consistency
dimensions.evidence: Hiring rubric exists but interviewers apply it inconsistently; IRR not measured.

weaknesses.text: No four-fifths analysis; disparate-impact exposure unmeasured.
No blind/anonymized screening; interviewer demographic skew unmitigated.
Unstructured keyword resume filter with no predictor validity evidence.

roadmap.title: Run four-fifths analysis on last 4 hiring cycles
roadmap.success_metric: Four-fifths ratio >= 0.80 for every protected group within 2 cycles

roadmap.title: Anonymize resumes (name, school, photo removal) pre-screen
roadmap.success_metric: 100% of resumes screened blind; demographic-cue detection audit passes

roadmap.title: Replace keyword filter with validated, job-related screening rubric
roadmap.success_metric: Predictor validity r >= 0.30 vs. on-the-job performance within 1 cycle

roadmap.title: Expand sourcing to women-in-tech and HBCU partnerships
roadmap.success_metric: Underrepresented applicants >= 35% of funnel within 2 cycles

bias_findings.mitigation: Cap referrals at 30%; add structured outreach to underrepresented communities

bias_findings.bias_vector: Affinity-based resume keywords; demographic cues visible
bias_findings.mitigation: Anonymize resumes; use validated, job-related screening rubric

bias_findings.bias_vector: Untrained interviewers; no calibration; like-me bias
bias_findings.mitigation: Structured questions, scoring rubric, calibration sessions, diverse panels

bias_findings.bias_vector: Unstructured debrief; halo effects from dominant interviewer
bias_findings.mitigation: Independent scoring before discussion; blind aggregation of scores

challenged.claim: Blind screening will fully remove bias
challenged.resolution: Pair blind screening with structured interviews and calibrated debriefs

challenged.challenge: Adverse-impact score (39) is critical and masks regulatory risk
challenged.resolution: Flag adverse-impact as a gating weakness regardless of total; require remediation before 'established' claim

sources: Uniform Guidelines on Employee Selection Procedures (29 CFR 1607) - https://www.eeoc.gov
Levashina et al. (2014), Personnel Psychology - https://doi.org/10.1111/peps.12109
Bohnet (2016), What Works: Gender Equality by Design
